until smooth and creamy, adding splashes of water as needed. Season generously with salt and pepper. Keep covered off heat until ready to⅓  serve.
4. While potatoes cook, heat a drizzle of oil in a medium, preferably ovenproof, pan (use a large pan for 4 servings) over medium-high heat. Add carrot; season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring,
until slightly softened, 2-3 minutes.

5. Add celery, onion, and a large drizzle of oil; season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring, until veggies are just tender, 5-7 minutes. TIP: If veggies begin to brown too quickly, add a splash of water.
6. Stir in garlic powder and 2 tsp chopped thyme; cook until fragrant, 30 seconds.
7. Add beef* to pan with veggies and season with salt and pepper. Cook, breaking up meat into pieces, until browned and cooked through, 4-6 minutes.
8. Add flour; cook, stirring, until thoroughly combined, 1 minute.

9. Add half the crushed tomatoes (all for 4 servings) and ½ cup water (¾ cup for 4) into pan with beef mixture. Stir in stock concentrates and bring to a boil. Cook until mixture is very thick, 1-2 minutes. Turn off heat.
10. Top beef filling with an even layer of mashed potatoes, leaving a gap around edge of pan. Evenly sprinkle with cheddar.
11. Broil until browned, 3-4 minutes.
12. Serve directly from pan.

name: Pumpkin Chai Loaf Cake
ingredients: 1 1/2 (150g) cups all-purpose flour, spooned and leveled*
directions: 1. Line a 9×5-inch loaf pan with parchment paper, ensuring it covers the bottom and sides for easy removal. Alternatively, generously grease the pan with butter or nonstick spray. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.

2. In a small saucepan over medium heat, melt the butter. Once melted, continue whisking the butter. You’ll notice the butter starting to foam; keep whisking until the butter gives off a nutty aroma, and the bits at the bottom start to brown. The butter will turn a golden brown color. Remove from the heat and transfer to a large bowl and let come to room temperature.

3. While the brown butter is cooling, mix together the dry ingredients. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, cardamom, ginger, nutmeg, allspice, cloves, and salt until well combined.
4. Once the brown butter has come to room temperature, whisk in the granulated and brown sugar until combined. Next, whisk in the eggs, vanilla, and pumpkin purée until fully combined.
5. Add the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients and fold everything together until no flour streaks remain. Fold in the milk until just incorporated.

6. Pour the pumpkin chai loaf cake batter into the prepared pan, smoothing out the top. Place in the oven to bake for 55-70 minutes. To check for doneness, insert a toothpick into the center of the loaf – if it comes out clean, the cake is done. Another way to check for doneness is to gently tap the center of the loaf cake, if it is still jiggly, it needs more time. If the cake springs back, it is ready.

7. While the loaf cake is baking, make the cream cheese frosting. In a bowl with a hand mixer or stand mixer, beat together the butter and cream cheese until smooth. Then, add in the vanilla extract and powdered sugar and beat until smooth and creamy. You can store in the fridge until you are ready to frost.
8. Allow the loaf cake to fully cool before frosting. Once cool, remove the cake from the pan. Spoon the cream cheese frosting on top of the cake, and spread it out with the back of a spoon.

name: Pumpkin Chocolate Chip Cookies
directions: ￼Brown butter in saucepan over medium heat for 7-10 minutes, then let cool to room temperature
￼Combine butter with brown sugar & granulated sugar
￼Add pumpkin purée & vanilla extract
￼Fold in flour, spices, baking soda & salt
￼Add as many chocolate chips as you like
￼Refrigerate door for minimum 30 minutes
￼Bake at 350 for 10-15minutes

name: Pumpkin Cinnamon Rolls
ingredients: 3/4 cup (180ml) milk of choice, warmed to 110F
1/2 cup (113g) salted butter, very softened (almost melted)
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
1/2 cup (120ml) heavy cream, for pouring over the rolls
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
directions: 1. Activate the yeast. Pour the warm milk into a small bowl or glass measuring cup. Add 1 tbsp (15g) of the brown sugar sugar and sprinkle the yeast overtop. Stir and allow to sit for 5-7 minutes, until frothy and activated.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, pumpkin pie spice, and cinnamon. Set aside.

3. Once activated, pour the yeast mixture into the bowl of a stand mixer. Add the rest of the sugar (1/2 cup minus the 1 tbsp that was used to activate the yeast), pumpkin puree, butter, egg, vanilla, and the flour mixture.
4. With the dough hook attachment (or with a wooden spoon if mixing by hand), mix on low speed for 1 minute, until barely combined. Rest for 5 minutes so the flour can soak up the liquids.

5. Then, mix on medium speed for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is elastic, smooth, and is pulling away from the sides of the bowl. If the dough seems a little too sticky, you can add 1 tbsp more flour at a time, until it starts to pull away from the sides of the bowl. Don’t add too much, you want the dough to be slightly sticky! I ended up adding about 1-2 tbsp more flour.
6. Transfer the dough to a lightly greased bowl. Cover and rise in a warm environment for 30-45 mins.
7. While the dough is rising, prepare the pumpkin spice cinnamon filling by mixing together all of the ingredients until well combined. Set aside.
8. Grease a 9x13” baking pan with butter.
9. Once the dough is done rising, generously flour your work surface. Turn the dough out onto the surface and lightly sprinkle the top with more flour.
10. If you are making 8 larger cinnamon rolls, roll the dough out to a 16x15” rectangle. For 12 smaller cinnamon rolls, roll it out to a 24x15” rectangle.
11. Spread/smooth the pumpkin spice cinnamon filling evenly all over the surface of the dough, leaving a very small border around the edges.
12. Starting on the long end, tightly roll the dough up into a log.
13. Using a sharp knife or unflavored floss, cut the dough into 8 larger or 12 smaller rolls total. Each roll should be ~2” in width.
14. Place the rolls into the prepared baking pan. Cover the pan and allow to proof for another 25-30 mins, until puffy.
15. Preheat the oven to 350F.
16. Once the rolls are proofed, warm the heavy cream in the microwave for 15 secs, and pour all over the rolls.
17. For 8 larger rolls, bake at 350F for the first 22-24 mins. Then, turn the oven temperature up to 375F and bake for another 5-7 mins, until the tops are golden and the rolls are cooked through.
18. For 12 smaller rolls, bake at 350F for the first 20-22 mins. Then, turn the oven temperature up to 375F and bake for another 5-7 mins.

19. While baking, prepare the cream cheese frosting. In a large bowl using a handheld mixer, beat the cream cheese and softened butter. Then, beat in the powdered sugar and vanilla until well combined and smooth.
20. Remove the rolls from the oven and rest for 10-15 mins.
21. Spread the cream cheese frosting all over the rolls.
22. Serve and enjoy!

name: Pumpkin Shaped Bagels
directions: 1. Combine water, salt, sugar & yeast. Cover with a towel & let stand for 10 minutes (until frothy)
2. Add flour & salt, mix dough until shaggy before moving to stand mixer
3. Knead dough for 3-4 minutes, then add food coloring
4. Continue to knead dough (10 minutes total)
5. Cut enough butchers twine for 8-10 bagels. Soak in butter or olive oil
6. Section dough into 8-10 rounds & shape into balls. Cover & let rest for 30 minutes
7. Boil water (optional: add tbsp maple syrup to water for added sweetness)
8. Wrap rounds in twine, cut excess at top
9. Boil for 1-1 1/2 minutes on each side
10. Cover in egg wash (optional: add toppings)
11. Bake at 400 for 30 minutes

name: Quick Stir Fry Noodles
directions: 1. Slice the snow peas, carrot, and bell pepper into thin strips. Mince 3 cloves of garlic and 1.5 tbsp of fresh ginger. Chop ½ an onion into small pieces. Cut the shrimp into bite-sized pieces. If using another protein, slice it accordingly.

2. Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add the ramen noodles and cook according to the package instructions. Drain and rinse the noodles under cold water to prevent them from sticking together. Set aside while you prepare the stir-fry.
3. In a small bowl, mix together 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp Shaoxing wine, 1 tsp sugar, 1 tsp white pepper, and 1 tbsp curry powder. Stir well to combine and set aside.

4. Heat 2 tbsp of coconut oil in a wok or large pan over high heat. Add the chopped shrimp and cook for about 30 seconds until they start to turn pink. Remove the shrimp from the pan and set aside—they will finish cooking later.
5. In the same pan, add the minced garlic and ginger. Stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant.
6. Add the sliced carrots, bell pepper, and snow peas. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes, keeping the heat high. Add the chopped onion last and cook for another minute until slightly softened.

7. Add the cooked noodles to the pan along with the shrimp. Pour the prepared sauce over the noodles and stir everything together. Toss and stir-fry for about 2 minutes until the noodles are evenly coated and heated through.
8. Remove from heat and garnish with chopped green onions if desired. Serve immediately and enjoy your quick and flavourful stir-fry noodles!

name: Quick Wonton Soup
ingredients: 3 green onions chopped (separate the white and light green parts from the dark green tops)
12 oz wontons I’m using frozen Trader Joe’s Chicken Cilantro Mini Wontons
Aunt Lily’s Chili Oil (This is a smaller batch than my original recipe calls for!)
½ cup neutral oil peanut, avocado, vegetable, or canola
directions: 1. Make the Chili Oil: Combine chili flakes, sesame seeds, garlic, ginger, sugar, salt, and a cinnamon stick in a heat-safe bowl. Heat the neutral oil in a small pan until hot (test by adding a small piece of garlic—if it sizzles, it's ready). Carefully pour the hot oil over the chili mixture and stir. Set aside to infuse. Skip this step if you already have chili oil on hand!
2. Melt coconut oil in a large pot. Add the white and light green parts of the green onions, the ginger, and the garlic. Sauté until fragrant.
3. Add in the chicken stock, bring to a bubble, then simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Add in the wontons along with the soy sauce, the fish sauce, and the rice vinegar (optional). Simmer for a few minutes until the wontons are heated through (I used pre-cooked).
5. Serve with the tops of the green onions and a big drizzle of chili oil!

name: Raspberry Chocolate Chip Cookies
ingredients: 2 sticks (1 cup) salted butter at room temperature
1 1/2 cups chopped chocolate chunks or chips
directions: 1. Preheat the oven to 350° F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.2. Add the butter to a skillet set over medium heat. Cook until the butter begins to brown, about 3-4 minutes. Remove from the heat and transfer to a heatproof bowl. Let cool 5 minutes or so. 3. To the brown butter, mix in the brown sugar, granulated sugar, eggs, and vanilla, mixing until smooth. Add the flour, baking soda, and salt. Gently fold in the chocolate and raspberries, do not overmix.4. Roll the dough into rounded tablespoon size balls and place 2 inches apart on the prepared baking sheet. The dough may be a little wet from the berries. Gently flatten the dough down. Bake 8 minutes. Remove from the oven, rotate and tap the baking sheet on the counter 1-2 times to flatten. Bake another 2-3 minutes or until the cookies are just beginning to set on the edges.5. Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet. They will continue to cook slightly as they sit on the baking sheet. Sprinkle with flaky salt (if desired). Eat warm (highly recommended) or let cool and store in an airtight container for up to 4 days.

name: Rigatoni with Beef & Zucchini Ragù
directions: Here’s the cleaned-up recipe:

1.	Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Once boiling, add rigatoni to pot. Cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente, 9-11 minutes. Reserve 1 cup pasta cooking water (1½ cups for 4 servings), then drain.
2.	Meanwhile, wash and dry produce.
3.	Halve, peel, and finely dice shallot. Peel and mince or grate garlic. Trim and grate zucchini on the largest holes of a box grater.

4.	Place zucchini in the center of a clean kitchen towel. Gather into a tight bundle and squeeze over the sink to remove as much liquid as possible. TIP: Alternatively, place grated zucchini in a fine-mesh strainer and press down with a spatula.

5.	Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add beef*, shallot, Italian Seasoning, a big pinch of salt, and pepper. Cook, breaking up meat into pieces, until browned and mostly cooked through, 3-5 minutes (it’ll finish cooking in the next step).
6.	Add tomato paste to pan with beef. Cook, stirring, until tomato paste is well combined and beef is cooked through, 1-2 minutes.
7.	Stir in zucchini, garlic, and a big pinch of salt. Cook, stirring, until zucchini is tender, 2-3 minutes.

8.	Stir in cream cheese, stock concentrate, ½ cup reserved pasta cooking water (ladle straight from the pot if pasta isn’t finished cooking yet), and ½ tsp sugar (for 4 servings, use ⅔ cup pasta cooking water and 1 tsp sugar). Simmer until sauce has slightly thickened, 1-2 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. TIP: If you’ve got some on hand, add a pinch of chili flakes from your pantry for a spicy kick.

9.	Reduce heat to medium low. Stir drained rigatoni, half the Parmesan, and 1 TBSP butter (2 TBSP for 4 servings) into pan with sauce until combined. TIP: If needed, stir in more reserved cooking water a splash at a time until pasta is coated in a creamy sauce.
10.	Divide pasta between plates. Top with remaining Parmesan and serve.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

name: Roasted Garlic Spaghetti with Seared Chicken
ingredients: ½ lb spaghetti (reserve 1 cup starchy pasta water)
Garnish: fresh chives or Italian parsley + extra parmesan
directions: 1. Preheat the oven to 400°F. Slice off the top ¼” of the head of garlic so that the cloves are exposed, drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil and wrap in tin foil. Roast in the oven for about 30 minutes, until the cloves are soft and slightly golden. Let cool until ready to use.
2. Pat dry the chicken breasts and season with garlic powder, salt, paprika, and oregano on both sides. Let them rest for 20-30 minutes, while the garlic is roasting.
3. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Sear the chicken breasts on each side for about 3-4 minutes, until cooked through (internal temp 165°F). Remove from the pan and set aside to rest.

4. While the chicken is searing, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the spaghetti until al dente, then reserve 1 cup of the starchy pasta water before draining. Set the al dente spaghetti aside.

5. Using the same pan you seared the chicken in so you get all of that flavour, reduce the heat to medium-low and melt the butter. Add the minced shallot and sauté for 3–4 minutes, until softened and lightly golden.
6. Stir in the tomato paste and cook until it deepens in colour, about 2 minutes.
7. Squeeze the roasted garlic cloves right out of the garlic bulb and into the pan. Stir to combine. Deglaze with the white wine and continue cooking for a few minutes until it has slightly reduced.

8. Add in the heavy cream and the parmesan and let this come together over medium heat, stirring gently to combine. Add the al dente spaghetti to the sauce, tossing gently and adding pasta water ¼ cup at a time until the sauce coats the noodles and reaches your desired consistency (you might not use the whole cup of pasta water).
9. Once the sauce has come together nicely, slice the chicken breasts into strips.
10. Serve the spaghetti into bowls, then top with the sliced chicken and a sprinkle of fresh chives/Italian parsley and some more parmesan, if you’d like.

name: Sausage & Veggie Skillet
ingredients: 4 Hatch chile sausages sliced into rounds (or any other sausage)
Garnish: a squeeze of lemon and fresh herbs (Italian parsley, cilantro, or chives)
directions: 1. Heat a drizzle of olive oil over medium heat, then add in the sliced sausages. Sauté until nicely browned, then remove from the pan and set aside.

2. In the same pan, add the chopped onion and let it soften. Next add in the chopped bell peppers and zucchini, along with your seasonings. Sauté for 2 minutes then add the garlic and sauté until fragrant. Remove the veggies from the pan and set aside.
3. Add pearl couscous to the pan, stirring for a minute so that it toasts lightly. Once toasted, add the chicken broth and let this come to a bubble, then cover fully and simmer for about 8 minutes.
4. Once the couscous has absorbed the liquid, add back in the sausage and veggies. Stir to combine, adjust seasonings to your liking, then serve with a squeeze of lemon and some fresh herbs!

name: Sheet Pan Greek Chicken
ingredients: Zest and juice of 1/2 a lemon
2 oz crumbled feta cheese and diced parsley for topping
directions: 1. Preheat oven to 425F.
2. Slice baby potatoes in half then add to a large sheet pan. Drizzle with olive oil, salt and pepper and toss to coat. Bake for 20 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, dice chicken into 1 inch cubes. Add to a large mixing bowl with greek yogurt, lemon juice and zest, minced garlic, onion powder, cumin, paprika, parsley, oregano, salt and pepper.
4. Cover and let marinate for 15 minutes while the potatoes finish baking.
5. Add the chicken to the sheet pan with the potatoes. Top with sliced red onion and lemon, then spray the top with olive oil spray.
6. Return to the oven for another 20 minutes, then I like to turn the oven to broil for 3-5 minutes until the tops of the chicken get a little char.
7. Remove from the oven, top with crumbled feta cheese and diced parsley, and enjoy!

name: Sheet Pan Ideas
directions: Cut vegetables and add to large bowl.
Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, honestly whatever you want.
Add drizzle of olive oil and mix. Add to sheet pan.
Bake at 425 for 20 minutes or until potatoes and meat are cooked.

name: Sheet Pan Sausage, Peppers & Potatoes
ingredients: 4 cooked sausages sliced into large diagonal slices
directions: 1. Preheat the oven to 400°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper for easy clean-up.

2. Prep the Veggies: Cut the potatoes into small chunks and slice the peppers and red onion. You can add the veggies right to your prepped sheet pan. Cut the lemon in half and set it aside. Slice the sausages into large diagonal pieces and set aside.
3. Assemble the Seasoning Mixture: In a small bowl, mix together the lemon pepper seasoning, garlic powder, celery salt, oregano, and paprika.
4. Drizzle veggies with a generous amount of olive oil, hit with seasonings and mix well to combine. Add the two lemon halves, cut side up, drizzling them with some olive oil as well.
5. Get this roasting in the oven for 20 minutes.
6. Stir veggies around and then add in the chopped sausage. Put this back into the oven for 15 more minutes. Optional: finish under the broiler for the last few minutes to get a nice golden char.

7. Once the roasted lemon has cooled slightly, squeeze one half of the roasted lemon into a bowl with mayonnaise and whole grain mustard. Season with black pepper and a tiny pinch of salt. Stir well to combine.

8. Spread a spoonful of the creamy mustard roasted lemon sauce onto each plate, and then add the sausage, peppers, onions, and potatoes on top. Garnish with fresh Italian parsley and the extra roasted lemon half!

name: Short Rib Rigatoni
ingredients: 1 fresh herb bundle (rosemary, thyme, basil – tied with kitchen twine)
directions: 1. Pat ribs dry, season with salt and pepper. Sear in olive oil on all sides until golden brown. Remove and set aside.
2. In the same pot, add onions and cook until soft. Stir in garlic, carrot, celery, and parsley. Cook until fragrant.
3. Pour in white wine, scraping the brown bits from the pan. Simmer for 2–3 minutes.
4. Stir in tomato paste, then pour in strained tomatoes and beef broth. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Mix well.
5. Return ribs to the pot. Add the tied herb bundle. Cover and braise at 325°F for about 4-5 hours, until meat is fork-tender.
6. Remove herb bundle. Take out ribs, shred meat, and return to sauce.
7. Boil rigatoni until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta water.
8. Toss pasta with braised beef sauce, adding reserved pasta water if needed. Stir in Parmesan until creamy. Serve immediately.

name: Shredded Chicken Sliders with Cowboy Butter
ingredients: ¼ cup mayonnaise or sour cream
Colby jack cheese slices or shredded
Zest of 1 lemon and juice of ½ lemon
directions: 1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.

2. Assemble the cowboy butter: Melt a stick of butter in the microwave, then stir in the minced garlic, chopped green onion and parsley, lemon zest and juice, Dijon mustard, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.
3. In a large bowl, mix together the shredded chicken with a few tablespoons of the melted butter mixture and the mayonnaise or sour cream. Set aside.
4. Slice the Hawaiian rolls in half horizontally and place the bottom half on a baking tray. Spread a little more mayo or sour cream on the cut side, then layer on the chicken mixture.
5. Top the chicken with slices of Colby Jack cheese, then place the top half of the rolls back on. Drizzle the remaining cowboy butter over the tops and brush to coat evenly.
6. Bake for about 15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the tops are golden. Serve warm, with extra cowboy butter for dipping!

name: Spiced Pork & Apple Cider Gravy
directions: Pat pork loin dry and rub with salt, pepper, cinnamon, cloves, paprika, garlic powder, and onion powder
Heat olive oil in dutch oven over medium high heat
Sear pork loin on all sides until golden brown. Remove and set aside
In the same pot, add carrots, potatoes, and purple onions. Sauce 5 mins until slightly browned.
Stirr in flour and cook for 1 minute to coat veggies.
Pour in apple cider and stir in dijon mustard. Scape up any brown bits from the bottom. Return loin to pot.
Cover dutch oven and bake at 325 for 1.5-2 hours, or until pork is tender and internal temp is at least 145 degrees.
Remove pork and let rest for 10 mins before slicing.
Spoon apple cider gravy and veggies over slices.

name: Steak Tacos with Pineapple Salsa
directions: 1.	Wash and dry produce.

2.	Halve, peel, and thinly slice onion; mince a few slices until you have 1 TBSP (2 TBSP for 4 servings). Drain pineapple. Dice tomato into ½-inch pieces. Mince jalapeño, removing ribs and seeds for less heat if desired.

3.	Pat steak* dry with paper towels and thinly slice against the grain. Heat a drizzle of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add steak, sliced onion, and Mexican Spice Blend to pan; season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is softened and steak is cooked to desired doneness, 3-6 minutes.

4.	Meanwhile, in a small bowl, combine drained pineapple, tomato, minced onion, vinegar, and as much jalapeño as you like. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Wrap tortillas in damp paper towels and microwave until warmed through, 30 seconds.
5.	Divide tortillas between plates and fill with steak mixture and salsa (draining first). Top tacos with sour cream. Serve.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

name: Taco Rice Bowls
ingredients: 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil (plus more as needed)
2 poblano peppers (cored, seeded, halved, and sliced)
1 tablespoon taco seasoning (either store-bought or homemade (see notes))
directions: 1. Prepare the pico de gallo: cut a few slices of red onion into small pieces until you have 3 tablespoons. Add to a medium-sized bowl along with the tomato, 1/4 cup cilantro, and jalapeno to taste (if using). Add juice from half the lime and a small pinch of lime zest. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
2. In a small bowl, combine the sour cream with juice from half the lime. Stir in a spoonful of water at a time until the sauce reaches a drizzling consistency. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

3. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and poblano peppers and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft and lightly caramelized, 4-5 minutes. Transfer to a bowl.

4. Add more oil to the pan if needed, then add the beef, taco seasoning, and a few shakes of salt and pepper. Stir while breaking the beef into small pieces with the spatula and cook until browned, 5-6 minutes. Taste and season with additional salt and pepper if needed.

5. Stir remaining lime zest into the cooked rice and season with salt and pepper. Divide between 4 bowls, then top each with the sauteed onions and peppers, ground beef, pico de gallo, sour cream, monterey jack cheese, and remaining cilantro.

name: Teriyaki Salmon Bowls
ingredients: 4 cups cooked white or brown rice (for serving)
directions: 1. Place the salmon filets in a shallow dish or a resealable plastic bag. In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce and pineapple juice. Pour over the salmon, gently turning over the salmon to make sure it’s evenly coated. Cover and place in the refrigerator for 30 minutes to marinate.
2. Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F and lightly grease a baking sheet with cooking spray or olive oil. Remove the fish from the marinade, patting dry with paper towels (save the marinade).
3. Bake the salmon for 12-15 minutes, or until it reaches desired doneness.

4. While the salmon is cooking, prepare the sauce. In a small saucepan, combine the soy sauce and pineapple juice marinade, brown sugar, rice vinegar, sesame oil, ginger, garlic, and red pepper flakes and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, in a small bowl combine the water and cornstarch. Whisk the mixture into the simmering soy sauce mixture and let it thicken. Remove from heat.
6. Divide the rice between 4 serving bowls and top each with salmon. Drizzle the sauce evenly on top. Top with cucumber, carrots, edamame, and scallions.

name: Toasted Orzo Potato & Leek Soup
ingredients: 1 leek just the white and light green parts, chopped
Toppings: creme fraiche sour cream, chives, salt & pepper
directions: 1. Chop the leek, using only the white and light green parts, and mince the shallot. Slice the garlic and cube the potato. Chop the bacon into small pieces.
2. In a large pot, cook the chopped bacon over medium heat until crispy. Once done, remove the bacon and set it aside on a paper towel. Leave about 1 tablespoon of bacon fat in the pot.
3. Add the dry orzo to the pot with the remaining bacon fat. Toast the orzo over medium heat until it turns golden brown, about 3-4 minutes. Remove the toasted orzo and set it aside.
4. Add 1 tablespoon of butter to the same pot. Once melted, sauté the chopped leek, shallot, and garlic for about 5 minutes until softened and fragrant.
5. Pour in the chicken stock and water. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then add the chopped potato and bay leaf. Cook for about 5 minutes.
6. Return the toasted orzo to the pot, and cook for another 10 minutes, or until the orzo and potatoes are tender. Remove the bay leaf.
7. Stir in the frozen peas and let the soup simmer for 2 minutes. Taste and adjust seasonings with salt and pepper as desired.
8. Ladle the soup into bowls and top with a dollop of creme fraiche or sour cream, the crispy bacon, and fresh chives. Serve and enjoy!

name: White Pasta e Fagioli
ingredients: 1 15 oz can of cannellini beans or borlotti beans
Garnish: grated parmesan cheese + fresh cracked black pepper
directions: 1. Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion, celery, and carrot. Cook for 7-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are softened and fragrant.

2. Add the garlic and rosemary, then cook for about 30 seconds, just until fragrant. Pour in the white wine to deglaze the pot, scraping up any bits from the bottom. Let it simmer for 2-3 minutes, until mostly reduced.

3. Next, add the rinsed beans, a pinch of salt, and a few cracks of black pepper. Stir to combine. Note: for a slight creaminess to the soup, reserve about ¼ of the beans. After you add the broth in the next step, blend the reserved beans with 1-2 ladles of broth until smooth, then stir the mixture back into the pot.
4. Pour in the broth and stir. Add the parmesan rind (if using), then bring this to a bubble.
5. Reduce to a simmer, then add in the ditalini pasta and stir occasionally until the pasta is tender. Remove the parmesan rind at this point if you used one.
6. Ladle into bowls, and finish with some grated parmesan cheese and extra freshly cracked black pepper if you like.
